hazard.summary: Persistent 429 on Garmin SSO login reproduces from plain curl across regions, indicating upstream edge or abuse-policy blocking rather than a client-side retry/backoff bug.
signals: persists even from browser/curl without any code

constraints: If a 429 reproduces from a minimal external probe outside the client, classify it as upstream availability or edge-policy failure before changing library retry logic.
Authentication flows must distinguish client misuse from upstream login-surface unavailability.
Login probes must be minimized to avoid amplifying possible edge or abuse-policy blocks.
verification: Reproduce the 429 from a minimal probe such as curl against the login endpoint without application code.
Confirm whether the failure occurs across multiple regions, networks, or Cloudflare edges.
Verify that existing saved-token flows still succeed while fresh login fails.
After mitigation or service recovery, verify that fresh login succeeds without changes to client retry logic.
notes: The evidence suggests this is not a local library implementation bug: the same 429 is returned from plain curl with no authentication headers, across multiple countries/providers, and persists across endpoint/version changes. This makes it a reusable example of external 429 misread as a client-side retry problem.

constraints: probe externally before patching retry logic
separate upstream availability failures from client retry bugs
anti_patterns: treating all 429s as local cooldown bugs
changing client retries before validating upstream behavior
using login endpoints as aggressive health checks
